steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage explains that weeds spread easily and quickly become unmanageable. The blank verb must describe an action that becomes difficult precisely because of this rapid, uncontrollable spread.

steps.title: Match the target meaning
steps.content:
Substitute each choice: ______ the spread of unwelcome weeds can be difficult because weeds spread easily and become unmanageable.

A) retrieving — "retrieving the spread" means recovering or fetching it. You retrieve a lost item, not a plant infestation. Nonsensical here. Eliminate.

B) saving — "saving the spread" is ambiguous but most naturally means preserving the spread — the opposite of what a gardener wants. Eliminate.

C) halting — "halting the spread" means stopping it. Because weeds "spread easily and quickly become unmanageable," stopping them is exactly what's difficult. Keep.

D) pretending — "pretending the spread" is grammatically broken; you pretend something, not a spread. Eliminate.

C is correct.
